mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending consumer spending weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and declining retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand despite aggregate output growth, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenue growth stalls.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth yields a coefficient insignificant at the 5% level in out-of-sample tests, or 60-day forward returns remain positive when the divergence occurs.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and elevated consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary supply shock environment, forcing higher discount rates and compressing profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy over a 24-month walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 250 reflect entrenched inflationary pressures that compel aggressive central bank tightening, raising the risk-free rate and equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative bias when CPIAUCSL crosses 250, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion and rising household income, directly supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when payroll growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with low federal funds rates indicate strong credit-fueled consumer demand and construction sector activity, forecasting positive S&P 500 forward returns through broad economic momentum.
failure_condition: The interaction between HOUST and FEDFUNDS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment signals a synchronized late-cycle economic expansion where capacity utilization peaks and labor markets tighten, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns before potential policy intervention.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative in > 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint signal fails to maintain a positive Rank IC above 0.03 over a 36-month walk-forward test.
